skill_candidates.name: phase-10-gad-eats-its-own-dogfood-planning-packs
skill_candidates.source_phase_title: GAD eats its own dogfood + planning packs

tasks.goal: Create .planning/ scaffold for get-anything-done and register in planning-config.toml.

Migrate gad-01 through gad-15 decisions from root DECISIONS.xml into vendor/get-anything-done/.planning/DECISIONS.xml. Add gad-16 (no subagents until evaluated) and gad-17 (context handoff via planning docs).
Add gad pack command that bundles .planning data into a repo-planner-compatible planning pack format (JSON bundle with state, phases, tasks, decisions, requirements, docRefs).
Create vendor/get-anything-done/AGENTS.md using GAD's own workflow — gad CLI commands, planning loop, context re-hydration, skill references.
Create skill stubs: skills/write-intent/SKILL.md, skills/write-feature-doc/SKILL.md, skills/write-tech-doc/SKILL.md.
Update gadConfig.load() to read [[docs.projects]] from planning-config.toml and expose as config.docsProjects. Update gad sink status and gad refs to include docs.projects entries.
Create docs-map-reader.cjs and DOCS-MAP.xml per planning root. Extend gad refs to include docs-map entries. Update gad sink compile to handle DOCS-MAP.xml.

Create eval documentation in sink: apps/portfolio/content/docs/get-anything-done/evals.mdx covering what GAD evals are, how they work, the DEFINITIONS.md model, cli-efficiency results, portfolio-bare concept.

skill_candidates.name: phase-12-kill-gsd-formalize-gad-loop-reduce-start
skill_candidates.source_phase_title: Kill GSD, formalize GAD loop, reduce startup redundancy

tasks.goal: Add decisions gad-17 (revised: auto-compact over fresh sessions), gad-18 (canonical loop: snapshot → work → update → commit), gad-19 (GSD is dead, GAD only).
Delete .claude/commands/gsd/ directory entirely (30 command files).

Rebrand .claude/hooks/ for GAD: remove gsd-check-update.js (checks npm for get-shit-done-cc). Rename gsd-context-monitor.js → context-monitor.js, strip GSD references from warning messages. Rename gsd-statusline.js → statusline.js, strip GSD update check and branding. Update .claude/settings.json to reference new hook filenames.

Simplify root AGENTS.md: remove the 15-file mandatory read order. Agents use gad snapshot on session start, not manual file reads. Remove GSD section. Keep build/lint/verify gates, conventions, site copy guide. Reference vendor/get-anything-done/AGENTS.md as the canonical loop definition.

Simplify .planning/AGENTS.md: remove redundant loop definition, trigger phrase table, and pattern examples that duplicate root AGENTS.md and vendor GAD AGENTS.md. Keep XML schema reference and planning-config.toml pointers. Reference gad-18 for the loop.

Update vendor/get-anything-done/AGENTS.md: mark as canonical loop source per gad-18. Update "Current work" section to reference phase 12. Fix gad-17 reference to reflect auto-compact over fresh sessions.

Uninstall GSD globally: delete ~/.claude/get-shit-done/, ~/.claude/commands/gsd/ (56 files), ~/.claude/hooks/gsd-*.js (5 files). Clean ~/.claude/settings.json — remove all GSD hooks and statusline, keep permissions and preferences.

Install all 56 GAD commands from vendor/get-anything-done/commands/gad/ as Claude Code slash commands in .claude/commands/gad/. Includes the full planning loop: plan-phase, execute-phase, discuss-phase, progress, check-todos, next, plus project lifecycle, debug, health, verify, stats, and all other commands.

Clean all GSD/get-shit-done references in vendor/get-anything-done/commands/gad/*.md and propagate to .claude/commands/gad/. Replace GSD→GAD, get-shit-done→get-anything-done, /gsd:→/gad: throughout. Note: 115 references to get-shit-done remain as literal directory/file paths (the actual folder is named that).

skill_candidates.name: phase-13-docs-commands-skill-content-docs-map-rol
skill_candidates.source_phase_title: Docs commands, skill content, DOCS-MAP rollout

tasks.goal: Extend gad docs command: add `gad docs list --projectid <id>` that shows all docs for a project — planning files from sink status, DOCS-MAP entries, and docs.projects entries. Add --all flag.

Create DOCS-MAP.xml for get-anything-done with entries for evals.mdx and any other non-planning feature docs in the sink. Reader (docs-map-reader.cjs) already exists — this creates the actual data files.
Create DOCS-MAP.xml for grime-time, repo-planner, and any other roots that have feature docs in their sink paths. Check each root's sink directory for non-planning MDX files.
Flesh out write-intent SKILL.md: add concrete examples, template output, edge cases. The skill should be usable by any agent reading it cold — currently too abstract.
Flesh out write-feature-doc SKILL.md: add concrete examples of good feature docs, MDX frontmatter template, sink path resolution logic. Currently steps are correct but thin.
Flesh out write-tech-doc SKILL.md: add concrete examples, mermaid diagram patterns, decision-referencing conventions. Currently steps are correct but thin.
CLI error hardening: audit all gad.cjs commands for gad-12 compliance (missing-arg UX prints choices + rerun hint). Test edge cases: missing .planning/ dirs, malformed XML, empty files.
Run gad sink compile to sync the 7 stale files (get-anything-done and grime-time). Verify sink is fully up to date after phase 12 changes.

skill_candidates.name: phase-14-eval-framework-escape-the-dungeon-tracin
skill_candidates.source_phase_title: Eval framework: escape-the-dungeon + tracing

tasks.goal: Create a gad eval project "escape-the-dungeon" from GAMEPLAY-DESIGN.xml and STAT-AND-BEHAVIOUR-TAXONOMY.md. Convert these source docs into a GAD REQUIREMENTS.xml in the eval project directory (evals/escape-the-dungeon/). The requirements should be structured for an agent to plan phases and implement from — not a raw XML dump.

Create bin/gad-tools.cjs — the GAD equivalent of gsd-tools.cjs. Provides the core commands that workflows depend on: init (phase-op, execute-phase, plan-phase), commit, config-get, config-set, state (load, update, record-session), phase (find, add, complete), roadmap (get-phase, analyze), generate-slug, current-timestamp, scaffold, agent-skills. Reads from XML planning files instead of MD. This replaces all gsd-tools.cjs references in workflows.

Define the eval trace format for real implementations: track which gad commands were called, which gad skills were triggered, arguments passed, token counts, time-to-completion. Extend TRACE.json schema beyond the current unit-coverage model to include tool_calls[] and skill_triggers[].

Define the eval scoring rubric for real implementations: CLI efficiency (same as v6), skill trigger accuracy (were the right skills triggered at the right time), planning quality (task coverage, state hygiene), and time-to-completion. Human feedback is a separate manual score added after.

Run the escape-the-dungeon eval: fresh agent session, agent reads requirements, plans phases, implements using the GAD loop, traces everything. Produce TRACE.json and SCORE.md. This validates the full loop end-to-end on a real project.
Run the portfolio-bare eval with updated tracing. Compare against prior runs. Tighten portfolio-bare based on findings from the escape-the-dungeon eval.

Review CONTEXT.md: what it is, how discuss-phase produces it, how downstream agents (researcher, planner) consume it. Evaluate whether gad CLI should have a `gad context-doc` command or if gad-tools.cjs scaffold is sufficient. Decide if CONTEXT.md should be a GAD skill output or a planning file type. Update skills and CLI to integrate CONTEXT.md into the loop if valuable.

skill_candidates.name: phase-14.5-eval-findings-enforce-the-loop-automate-
skill_candidates.source_phase_title: Eval findings: enforce the loop, automate tracing, auto-scaffold conventions

tasks.goal: Create eval template AGENTS.md that enforces the GAD loop. The eval template's .planning/ directory must include an AGENTS.md that tells the agent: (1) run gad snapshot first, (2) plan phases in ROADMAP.xml before coding, (3) update TASK-REGISTRY.xml per task, (4) update STATE.xml after each phase, (5) capture decisions. This file is what the agent reads to know how to work — not a prompt instruction it ignores.

Update eval-run skill/command to inject snapshot context INTO the agent prompt. Instead of telling the agent "run gad snapshot", the eval-run workflow should run the snapshot itself and include the output in the agent's initial context. The agent starts with context, not instructions to get context.

Create gad eval trace reconstruct --project <name> command. Parses git log + planning doc state to build TRACE.json after the fact. Detects: which files were created/modified per commit, which planning docs were updated, time between commits (phase duration), task completion count from TASK-REGISTRY.xml diff. This replaces manual log-cmd/log-skill with post-hoc reconstruction.

Add checkpoint enforcement to execute-phase workflow. Before the agent can start the next task, it must have: (1) updated TASK-REGISTRY.xml for the previous task, (2) updated STATE.xml next-action. The workflow checks these preconditions. If not met, it prompts the agent to update before continuing. Not a hook — a step in the workflow that reads the planning docs and verifies.

Auto-scaffold CONVENTIONS.md after first implementation phase of greenfield projects. When phase 01 (or first phase with code output) completes in a project with no existing CONVENTIONS.md, run a lightweight codebase scan (file structure, naming patterns, import style, content pack format) and write .planning/CONVENTIONS.md. Integrate with gad:map-codebase skill output.

Define expected_triggers for implementation evals. The TRACE.json skill_accuracy field needs expected_triggers populated before the eval runs — defining which skills SHOULD fire and when. Create a template expected_triggers list for implementation evals: discuss-phase before planning, plan-phase before execution, execute-phase per phase, verify-work after phases, map-codebase after first implementation.

Re-run escape-the-dungeon eval with fixes applied (template AGENTS.md, snapshot injection, checkpoint enforcement). Compare v3 TRACE.json against v2. Validate that: gad CLI is actually used, task-level tracking happens, conventions are generated, trace has real data not backfilled.

skill_candidates.name: phase-17-reverse-engineer-skill-reader-workspace-
skill_candidates.source_phase_title: Reverse-engineer skill + reader-workspace eval

tasks.goal: Run gad:reverse-engineer on vendor/repub-builder/src/reader to produce REQUIREMENTS.xml, DECISIONS.xml, CONVENTIONS.md, ROADMAP.xml for the reader-workspace eval.
Create reader-workspace eval project with REQUIREMENTS.md, template/, and AGENTS.md.
Run reader-workspace eval v1: fresh agent builds the EPUB reader from reverse-engineered requirements alone. Produce TRACE.json and SCORE.md. Validates the full reverse-engineer → implement cycle.
Score reader-workspace v1: requirement coverage (51 criteria), architecture alignment (12 decisions), state hygiene, convention adherence. Compare output against reference implementation quality.

skill_candidates.name: phase-18-eval-suite-parallel-runs-iteration-cycle
skill_candidates.source_phase_title: Eval suite — parallel runs + iteration cycle

tasks.goal: Replace the stub in `gad eval run` with a prompt generator: read the eval template (AGENTS.md, REQUIREMENTS.xml, DECISIONS.xml, CONVENTIONS.md, ROADMAP.xml, STATE.xml), construct a full bootstrap prompt, and output it to stdout + eval-output.txt. The user/agent copies this prompt into their AI tool. Agent-agnostic per gad-01.

Add `gad eval suite` CLI command: lists all eval projects with templates, generates bootstrap prompts for each, outputs them as separate files in a timestamped suite run directory. Shows a summary table of what will be run.

Add `gad eval report` cross-project comparison: reads TRACE.json from latest run of each project, produces a comparison table (phases, tasks, discipline, scores) across all projects. Supports --all flag for all projects or specific --project list.

Add comprehensive logging: GAD CLI call logger (JSONL per day), PostToolUse hook for tool tracing (Bash/Read/Write/Edit/Agent/Skill/Grep/Glob), `gad log show|stats|clear` commands, `gad eval trace from-log` for definitive trace building from actual logs, GAD_LOG_DIR env for eval-scoped logs.
Run the full eval suite: portfolio-bare v3, escape-the-dungeon v4, reader-workspace v2. Launch all three in parallel worktree agents. Reconstruct traces and produce cross-project report.
Iterate on findings: review eval results, tighten at least one skill based on patterns, update eval-suite SKILL.md with lessons learned, add any new eval criteria discovered.

skill_candidates.name: phase-20-real-time-tooling-mcp-bridge-and-tooling
skill_candidates.source_phase_title: Real-time tooling, MCP bridge, and tooling evals

tasks.goal: Document tooling eval contract: extend TRACE.json with eval_type tooling|mcp, trace_schema_version, nested tooling or mcp metrics blocks, optional metrics.json for granular histograms. Record gad-26–gad-28 in DECISIONS.xml. Add a short section to vendor/get-anything-done/AGENTS.md (or evals/README) describing implementation vs tooling evals and how regressions are compared.

Implement `gad dev` or `gad watch planning`: debounced watch on .planning/**/*.xml (configurable), invoke existing refs verify logic, print one JSON summary line per run (ok, missing paths, timing) for scripts and terminal/CI consumers. Optional --poll for unreliable FS watchers. No network by default.

Add evals/tooling-watch-baseline (or equivalent): fixed fixture repo with known good/bad paths, automated Node harness that starts watcher, performs scripted edits, asserts correctness + latency percentiles, writes evals/<project>/vN/TRACE.json with eval_type tooling. Runnable in CI without an LLM.

Harden implementation eval scoring automation: generate scores.json alongside SCORE.md from score-generator; change gad eval scores to prefer JSON totals over SCORE.md regex. Prepare same pattern for tooling composite (tooling_composite) so weights can evolve without breaking old runs.

Optional MCP server package: thin adapter over the same core as CLI (verify, snapshot summary, session status). Stdio MCP; document Cursor config. Agents may use it; primary user remains human IDE workflows.
MCP tooling eval: golden tool invocations, cold-start and per-tool latency, error rate on bad args. Produces eval_type mcp TRACE.json; compare across gad releases.
IDE integration: document VS Code / Cursor Tasks (background watch), optional problem matcher mapping JSON errors to Problems panel. Update portfolio sink evals.mdx when behavior is stable.

Extend gad eval report and gad eval diff to include tooling/mcp runs (filter by eval_type, compare tooling.metrics and composite). Ensure suite or follow-up command can run implementation + tooling evals in one report.

skill_candidates.name: phase-21-eval-framework-evolution-v3-scoring-gate
skill_candidates.source_phase_title: Eval framework evolution: v3 scoring, gates, preservation contract, greenfield/brownfield

tasks.goal: Fix MCP UTF-8 buffer bug: Content-Length counts bytes but handleData used string.length. tools/list responses with em dashes broke the parser. Switch to Buffer throughout MCP harness and server.
Speed up planning-ref-verify by reading from planning-config roots instead of full tree walk, plus cache fs.statSync results.

Standardize implementation eval composite formula. Replace inconsistent per-eval weights with a single v3 formula: requirement_coverage + planning_quality + per_task_discipline + skill_accuracy + time_efficiency + human_review. Document in DEFINITIONS.md.
Clean skill_accuracy: remove methodology behaviors (decisions-captured, multi-phase-planning, phase-completion, task-lifecycle) from expected_triggers. Only actual /gad:* skill invocations count.
Add gad eval review CLI command to record human review scores that get folded into the composite with the v3 weights.
Add gad eval open CLI command to serve eval build output via local HTTP server (file:// doesn't work for ES module bundles from Vite/KAPLAY).
Create escape-the-dungeon-bare eval project: same game requirements, no GAD framework, agent creates own workflow. Baseline A/B comparison for the freedom hypothesis.
Create escape-the-dungeon-emergent eval project: no framework, but inherits skills from the previous run. Tests whether self-created systems improve iteratively.
Version game requirements. Document v1→v2→v3 history inside REQUIREMENTS.xml. Each eval round should reference the requirements version it used.

Build requirements v3: add gate criteria (game loop, spell crafting, UI quality) that override requirement_coverage to 0 if failed. Trim source gameplay design doc from 640 → 120 lines for the vertical slice focus.
Run round 2 greenfield evals with v2 requirements. Three conditions: GAD v7, Bare v2, Emergent v1.
Run round 3 greenfield evals with v3 requirements (gates). GAD v8, Bare v3, Emergent v2.
Document the freedom hypothesis finding: GAD framework constraint correlates inversely with creative output quality on greenfield game implementation. Capture in FINDINGS doc and decision.
Build eval preservation contract: gad eval preserve command that copies code+planning+build+logs to canonical per-version locations. Enforce via tests/eval-preservation.test.cjs.
Fix eval preserve to copy the ENTIRE project (not cherry-picked files). Agent may create files we don't anticipate.
Mandate game/.planning/ as the home for all eval workflow artifacts regardless of framework. Bare and emergent must put workflow files there too, not at project root.
Add gad worktree management commands: list, show, clean, prune. User had no visibility into .claude/worktrees/agent-* directories.
Categorize evals by eval_mode (greenfield|brownfield) and workflow (gad|bare|emergent). Show in gad eval list.

Create three brownfield eval projects (etd-brownfield-gad/bare/emergent) using bare v3 as baseline. Shared extension requirements (_brownfield-extensions-v4.md) describing v4 features (floor progression, respawning encounters, forge affinity, skills system, visual upgrade).
Port session tracking into TASK-REGISTRY.xml instead of Claude Code Task tool. Stop using ephemeral session tasks for GAD-tracked work.
Create an experiment log doc (evals/EXPERIMENT-LOG.md) that tracks every round: conditions, requirements version, results, findings. Running ledger across all rounds.

Build requirements v4: pressure-oriented design (not features checklist). Authored dungeon with floors → 5-8 rooms → boss gate. Room types: Combat, Elite, Forge, Rest, Event. Each floor must introduce a mechanical constraint that can't be brute-forced with default spells. Forge is gate with at least one encounter per floor that favors crafted/adapted spells. Pressure mechanics required from at least 2 of 4 categories. Starter abilities explicitly insufficient. Skills scored but not gate (combat needs non-spell action category only). Sprites: attempt sourcing first, coherent fallback allowed. Traits label in UI, narrativeStats in code. Add ingenuity/adaptation scoring dimension.

Create create_skill skill doc (skills/create-skill/SKILL.md) that documents how bare/emergent agents can define their own reusable skills. Only skill bare starts with; emergent starts with create_skill + inherited skills from previous runs.

Rewrite bare and emergent AGENTS.md to be truly minimal. Stop mandating ARCHITECTURE.md, CHANGELOG.md, or any specific artifact. Only mandate: (1) all workflow artifacts in game/.planning/, (2) reference create_skill skill for defining new skills. Everything else is agent-defined. This defeats the previous round's too-prescriptive guidance and lets us observe what emergent workflows actually produce.

Create gad:find-sprites skill (skills/find-sprites/SKILL.md) that documents the asset sourcing workflow: web search for free/open-license sprites and icons, preference order (npm packs → web search → generated → geometric fallback), integration tips, license verification. Referenced by v4 AGENTS.md for all 3 greenfield conditions.
Run round 4 greenfield with v4 requirements. Three conditions: GAD, Bare, Emergent. All with pressure-oriented gates.

Retry GAD greenfield v11 after HTTP 529 investigation. First attempt at v10 (tool_uses=55) completed phases 01+02 (scaffold + data layer) before Anthropic API returned overloaded_error. Previous attempt crashed earlier at tool_uses=18. Pattern suggests GAD's setup-heavy phases (snapshot + planning XML writes) spend proportionally more time in server-dependent states during peak load periods. Investigation needed: (1) check Anthropic status page for correlation with our crash timestamps; (2) test whether spreading the snapshot + plan calls across shorter API invocations reduces 529 exposure; (3) consider a retry-with-backoff wrapper in the eval runner for 529 specifically (not for rate limits). Only retry GAD v11 after investigation produces a mitigation or explicit "try again, the API was just overloaded that hour" conclusion.
Run round 1 brownfield experiments. Three conditions using bare v3 baseline + v4 extensions. BLOCKED on portfolio documentation (21-28).

skill_candidates.name: phase-22-portfolio-experiment-documentation-hub-p
skill_candidates.source_phase_title: Portfolio experiment documentation — hub, per-project, per-run, findings, graphs

tasks.goal: Plan the portfolio experiment documentation structure — pivoted to a standalone Next.js site at vendor/get-anything-done/site/ instead of apps/portfolio docs. Designed single-page sections (workflow, rounds, projects, results, playable, requirements, catalog, templates) plus dynamic per-run pages.
Hub/home page explaining the experiment, freedom hypothesis, methodology, and linking to every section.
Per-eval-project cards for all six projects with greenfield/brownfield split.
Per-run detail pages for every eval run with TRACE.json breakdown, dimension scores, gate report, process metrics, playable link.
Per-round experiment summary sections that compare conditions.

Visualizations/graphs across rounds. Deferred — score bars on the per-run page and run cards cover the "at a glance" comparison for now; a dedicated charts section can be revived later if we decide we're missing the signal.
Requirements version history (v1 → v4 lineage with diffs).

Decisions index page. Deferred — decisions referenced inline in Rounds and Requirements sections; full decisions index can be a dedicated /decisions route later when the list is big enough to warrant it.
Findings as standalone pages: /findings index + /findings/[slug] per finding.
Build pipeline so the site data stays in sync with the repo.
Extract v1 and v2 REQUIREMENTS.md snapshots from git history.
Markdown rendering via marked in prebuild.
Per-skill detail pages app/skills/[id]/page.tsx.
Per-agent detail pages app/agents/[id]/page.tsx.
Per-command detail pages app/commands/[id]/page.tsx.
Findings routes at /findings and /findings/[slug].
Framework vs eval-inherited catalog split.
/planning meta-transparency page.
skills/portfolio-sync/SKILL.md — methodology for keeping the public site in sync with evals.

Dedicated /gad framework overview page. Currently the home Loop + Catalog sections are entry points but there is no standalone "what is GAD" reference page someone can deep-link to. The catalog cards work but users have asked for a top-level GAD explainer + index that links into skills/agents/commands as a single navigation hub.

/methodology page that explains how tracing works, what fields TRACE.json captures, all scoring formulas per eval project (pulled from evals/<project>/gad.json), the low-score caps from v3, and the gate logic. This is the "how we measure" appendix users have been asking for. Includes concrete worked examples showing how a composite score was derived.

Per-run page enhancements: (1) render composite formula as a weighted-sum breakdown showing each dimension's contribution to the composite; (2) when skill_accuracy has expected_triggers array (like v5), render the per-skill trigger table; (3) when skill_accuracy is a bare number (like v8), show a "tracing gap" callout noting the run didn't capture per-skill detail and link to phase 25 (trace schema overhaul).

Per-project detail pages at /projects/[id]. Shows: project description + mode + workflow, the catalog scope for that project (framework GAD = full catalog, bare = 2 bootstrap skills, emergent = 2 bootstrap + inherited), all runs with inline scores, "what the agent built for itself" per run (scan run/.planning/skills/*.md and similar).

Lightweight SVG charts in a Graphs section on the home page: composite + human review bars per run grouped by workflow, rounds timeline, freedom hypothesis scatter (composite vs human review divergence). No charting library — pure React-rendered SVG so it ships in every prebuild.
Nav surface update: add Framework + Methodology + Projects links. Current nav is already packed — may need a secondary nav row or drop items.

Expose scoring weights per project from evals/<project>/gad.json in the generated data. Every eval project can have different weights; we're currently only reading one set for display. Emit SCORING_WEIGHTS by project so the methodology page and per-run formula display can show the project-specific math.

New skill skills/framework-upgrade/SKILL.md. Methodology for versioning the GAD framework on branches (version/v1.x), stamping every TRACE.json with the framework commit it ran against, re-running past evals when the framework changes to check for framework-level score drift. References phase 25 as the backing work.
Mark the create-skill inheritance requirement explicit in a DECISION so bare/emergent templates are not accidentally regressed.

/lineage page explaining context rot, the GSD upstream, the RepoPlanner predecessor (b2gdevs / https://repo-planner.vercel.app/ — first Ralph Wiggum loop formalization), and how GAD combines both approaches plus measurement. Links to both projects' sources. Captures decision gad-56.

Agent Runtimes comparison table on /methodology showing which coding agents can produce trace v4 data (Claude Code ✓ via hooks, Aider ✓ via Python callbacks, Continue.dev ✓ via extension API, Cursor ✗, vanilla Codex CLI ✗ stream-only). Published alongside decision gad-53 explicit note that agents without hook runtimes are unsupported for GAD evaluation.

Phase 26 scaffolding: install @remotion/player, create site/remotion/ directory with Root.tsx composition registry + one placeholder Composition.tsx (the v8 dissection stub), create app/videos/page.tsx index with filter, create components/VideoEmbed.tsx inline player wrapper (30s cap, cinematic passthrough, stop button, reuses existing site components as frames). Does NOT author full video content — the scaffold establishes the pattern and the first composition is a placeholder.
Write .planning/DISCUSS-PHASE-25.md capturing the trace schema v4 discussion output.

`gad install` CLI subcommand as a local-checkout-friendly wrapper around existing bin/install.js. The underlying install.js already supports --claude --cursor --codex --opencode --gemini --copilot --antigravity --windsurf --augment --all --uninstall and ships the full GSD-pattern cross-runtime install — but it's hardcoded for the npm-published package layout (expects to find files under `node_modules/get-anything-done/get-anything-done/`). This task wraps it in a `gad install` subcommand that resolves paths correctly whether running from a git checkout (vendor/get-anything-done/) or an npm install. Related to 22-34 (fix install.js directly) — this task may become a no-op if 22-34 fixes the path resolution at the install.js level.
Fix path resolution bug in bin/install.js for local git checkouts.
Design trace event envelope + 4 event types as a reusable library in lib/trace-schema.cjs.
Write the hook handler script at bin/gad-trace-hook.cjs that Claude Code invokes for PreToolUse/PostToolUse.
4 KB truncation helper with head+tail split for trace event outputs.
`gad install hooks` CLI subcommand that wires the trace hook into Claude Code settings.json.

Fix YAML parsing errors in session-authored skill frontmatter (create-skill, objective-eval-design). Descriptions with embedded colons like "methodology:" and "discipline:" were parsed by js-yaml as key-value separators, breaking skill loading in Claude Code. Fix by converting plain scalar descriptions to YAML folded block scalar form (`description: >- ...`) which treats the body as opaque.

Dogfood-failure recovery: author skills/emergent/session-discipline/SKILL.md as an agent-authored hard-start checklist. Move agent-authored skills into skills/emergent/ so default install can skip them. Add origin + authored-by + authored-on + excluded-from-default-install fields to CatalogSkill. Capture gad-78 (dogfood failure) + gad-79 (task_pressure programmatic) decisions.

Reorganize .planning/ root: move ad-hoc markdown docs (ASSUMPTIONS.md, GAPS.md, SKEPTIC.md, SESSION-REFLECTION-*.md) into a new .planning/docs/ subdirectory per user directive. Rationale: .planning/ root should hold the formal XML artifacts that gad snapshot surfaces; ad-hoc human-written docs that are not formal (but still project-relevant) belong in a peer docs/ folder. Update all site page references to the new paths.

Retroactively update TASK-REGISTRY.xml to mark 22-20/22-21/22-23/22-24/22-25/22-29 as done (work shipped in prior sessions without registry updates — dogfood failure per gad-78). Add new tasks 22-36/22-37/22-38/22-39/22-40/22-41/22-42/22-43 for this session's work.

Agent-crawlable documentation export: emit public/llms.txt (concise index per llmstxt.org convention), public/llms-full.txt (full-text dump of skills/decisions/questions/bugs/glossary/tasks/phases/current-requirements), public/api/docs.json (structured JSON with schema gad-docs-v1) at prebuild. Provides a full API reference for external agents evaluating or reviewing GAD without needing to crawl HTML.

Interactive chart on /roadmap showing human review across rounds broken out by hypothesis track. Solid lines for tracks with real data (freedom/csh/gad). Dashed ghost lines for planned tracks without runs yet (content-driven, codex runtime) so the research plan is visible.

/hypotheses index page + /freedom evidence page + /content-driven planned-hypothesis page. Every hypothesis the project tracks gets wired to its eval track, its latest evidence, and its dedicated evidence page. Status labels: preliminary observation / discussing / operationalized / not-yet-tested.

Clickable skills modal on /emergent: convert the static skill-artifact chip list on the emergent page into client-component buttons that open a modal showing the full SKILL.md content with Copy button + GitHub source link. Per user directive 2026-04-09: "I wish i could click and download or get that pop up of those skills on the page."

Scaffold evals/gad-explainer-video/ as a new eval project: REQUIREMENTS.md describing the Remotion GAD explainer video task (3-minute explainer, 4 mandatory gate frames, pedagogical clarity as primary rubric dimension), gad.json with workflow:gad + compare_to bare/emergent siblings + rubric weights. Tests freedom/CSH/emergent-evolution hypotheses on a DIFFERENT task domain (video composition instead of game implementation) — the generalization test /skeptic says we need.

Manual fresh-clone contribution test (open question fresh-clone-contribution-test, decision gad-77). Clone the repo into a new directory, walk through the /contribute 5-step flow (clone → install → open in Claude → talk conversationally), document everything that breaks, update /contribute with the friction points, remove the "fresh-clone test still open" amber warning. Goal: make contribution actually work for a human who has never touched GAD before.

Site/remotion/ runtime scaffold (second half of task 22-31): create Root.tsx composition registry, one placeholder Composition.tsx, site/app/videos/page.tsx loading compositions via @remotion/player, VideoEmbed.tsx wrapper component. This unblocks the gad-explainer-video eval project (22-43) being run for the first time.

Adopt agentskills.io .agents/skills/ convention (gad-80). Extend bin/install.js to copy skills to BOTH .claude/skills/ (native) AND .agents/skills/ (cross-client) for supported runtimes. Migrate the fundamental triumvirate (create-skill, merge-skill, find-skills per gad-73) to the new location once those skills exist. Required for the codex-vs-claude comparison eval (task 89) because Codex uses different discovery conventions — if we target .agents/skills/, both runtimes find the same skills. Also honor the excluded-from-default-install:true frontmatter flag I added to the emergent skill — the installer must skip those.

Build a /standards page on the site citing both the Anthropic skills guide (resources.anthropic.com/hubfs/The-Complete-Guide-to-Building-Skill-for-Claude.pdf) and agentskills.io. Link from /skills, /security, /methodology, /gad. The page must summarize: (a) SKILL.md format + required frontmatter, (b) progressive disclosure three-tier loading, (c) name collision handling, (d) the per-skill evaluation methodology (with_skill vs without_skill baseline per agentskills.io/skill-creation/evaluating-skills). This becomes the reference page every skill-related section of the site cites instead of repeating the content inline.

Worktree isolation audit script (gad-82, open question worktree-isolation-verification). Write scripts/audit-worktree-isolation.mjs that walks a fresh eval worktree and reports: which files from the parent repo are reachable (by relative path traversal), what settings from .claude/settings.json inherit, what env vars are set, what's on PATH, which workspace skills at vendor/get-anything-done/skills/ could be picked up by a scan that walks parent dirs. Output structured report. ROUND 5 IS BLOCKED on either passing this audit or documenting which parent-repo state is explicitly allowed (e.g. the trace hook handler is allowed because it's the instrumentation layer per gad-50; workspace skills are NOT allowed because bare condition requires zero framework affordances).

Skill collision detection at prebuild (gad-81). Scan every skills/*/SKILL.md description for overlapping trigger keywords. Compute a keyword profile per skill, find overlaps above a threshold, emit a collision report to data/skill-collisions.json. Add a "collision candidates" section to the /skills site page showing any detected overlaps with a merge-skill suggestion link (merge-skill itself not yet built — link falls back to a manual-review prompt). Also: a keyword index for every skill so users searching can see what terms trigger each.

Extract thinking blocks from session.jsonl into TRACE.json derived.reasoning_trace (gad-84, GAPS G13). Post-hoc offline enrichment — no new hooks required. Walk a run's session.jsonl, pull every thinking block and inter-tool message text, attach to TRACE.json with timestamps tying thoughts to subsequent tool uses. First exploratory analysis: thought-to-action interval, thought length distribution, keyword presence (exploration vs execution), thought-before-action vs thought-after-action ordering. This is the highest-leverage way to get "agent reasoning" signal without Claude Code exposing new hook surfaces.

Scaffold new eval domain evals/skill-evaluation-app/ per decision gad-88. Third task-domain alongside escape-the-dungeon (game dev) and gad-explainer-video (video composition). Tests the same hypotheses (freedom / CSH / emergent-evolution) on GUI app development. The GUI, once built, is also part of the GAD eval framework (dogfood).

Build the GAD-native per-skill evaluation harness: gad eval skill <name> CLI subcommand. Reads evals/evals.json inside the skill directory, spawns clean-context subagent runs (with_skill + without_skill) via gad worktree, captures trace events from v4 schema (gad-50) as the observable signal, grades assertions by querying trace events + file mutations + commit log (NOT LLM self-report — that's what skill-creator's Python harness does and it didn't work for us), emits benchmark.json compatible with agentskills.io format. This is the load-bearing piece that lets every GAD skill graduate from experimental to canonical per gad-86. Directly answers programmatic-eval GAPS G2 + the hygiene half of G11.

Author merge-skill + find-skills SKILL.md files as workspace skills. find-skills is a thin methodology wrapper around npx skills (vercel-labs/skills CLI per gad-85). merge-skill is a pure methodology doc for fusing overlapping skills. Both start as experimental (status: experimental) because no evaluation harness has been run against them yet.

Landing page + /hypotheses interactive chart. User directive 2026-04-09: "the interactive chart I asked for should have been on the front page, but I dont see it... I still see an emergent page, but not a hypothesis page with graphs and etc detailing it out visually." Move or duplicate the HypothesisTracksChart from /roadmap onto the landing page (after Hero, before WhatItIs) and add it to /hypotheses above the hypothesis cards.

skill_candidates.name: phase-30-eval-framework-v2-domains-tech-stacks-ro
skill_candidates.source_phase_title: Eval framework v2 — domains, tech stacks, rounds per project

tasks.goal: gad rounds --project shows per-project rounds derived from TRACE.json requirements_version changes across runs.
Build requirement gate — gad eval score checks if build requirement (from gad.json) was produced. Failed gate = eval incomplete.
Human time estimation in eval prompt — buildEvalPrompt() includes instruction for agent to estimate human_estimate_hours before coding.
Source + build size computation — gad eval preserve computes source_size_bytes and build_size_bytes, writes to TRACE.json.
Site project detail shows domain badge + tech stack from eval project metadata.

skill_candidates.name: phase-34-executable-release-pipeline-github-first
skill_candidates.source_phase_title: Executable release pipeline + GitHub-first distribution
skill_candidates.reviewed_notes: test discard — phase 34 release work is one-off, not a reusable pattern

tasks.goal: Define the executable packaging target for gad: Windows, macOS, and Linux standalone binaries with no Node requirement. Choose and document the packaging toolchain, output artifact names, signing/notarization assumptions, and which runtime files (CLI bundle, hooks, templates, skills) must ship alongside the executable.

Implement the official GitHub-first install flow in docs and CLI messaging. Primary path: GitHub Release executable. Secondary path: direct GitHub repo/Node install. Remove or rewrite remaining npm-first copy in quick start, README, starter READMEs, and site pages.

Publish installable GAD skills through skills.sh / `npx skills add` using the GitHub repo as the source of truth. Document the exact command(s), verify the repo is listable/installable from skills.sh, and make skill installation distinct from CLI installation throughout the docs.

Build a path-gated release workflow so executable rebuild/release work only triggers on shipped CLI surface changes (bin/, lib/, hooks/, package.json, build/release scripts, and bundled assets). Non-shipping docs/site/eval/planning commits must not require a new release.

Implement the release pipeline: Windows authoritative local build, optional GitHub Actions builds for macOS/Linux, and GitHub Release asset publication/update. Document the operator flow for releasing after CLI-shipping changes.

skill_candidates.name: phase-10-gad-eats-its-own-dogfood-planning-packs
skill_candidates.source_phase_title: GAD eats its own dogfood + planning packs

tasks.goal: Create .planning/ scaffold for get-anything-done and register in planning-config.toml.

Migrate gad-01 through gad-15 decisions from root DECISIONS.xml into vendor/get-anything-done/.planning/DECISIONS.xml. Add gad-16 (no subagents until evaluated) and gad-17 (context handoff via planning docs).
Add gad pack command that bundles .planning data into a repo-planner-compatible planning pack format (JSON bundle with state, phases, tasks, decisions, requirements, docRefs).
Create vendor/get-anything-done/AGENTS.md using GAD's own workflow — gad CLI commands, planning loop, context re-hydration, skill references.
Create skill stubs: skills/write-intent/SKILL.md, skills/write-feature-doc/SKILL.md, skills/write-tech-doc/SKILL.md.
Update gadConfig.load() to read [[docs.projects]] from planning-config.toml and expose as config.docsProjects. Update gad sink status and gad refs to include docs.projects entries.
Create docs-map-reader.cjs and DOCS-MAP.xml per planning root. Extend gad refs to include docs-map entries. Update gad sink compile to handle DOCS-MAP.xml.

Create eval documentation in sink: apps/portfolio/content/docs/get-anything-done/evals.mdx covering what GAD evals are, how they work, the DEFINITIONS.md model, cli-efficiency results, portfolio-bare concept.

skill_candidates.name: phase-12-kill-gsd-formalize-gad-loop-reduce-start
skill_candidates.source_phase_title: Kill GSD, formalize GAD loop, reduce startup redundancy

tasks.goal: Add decisions gad-17 (revised: auto-compact over fresh sessions), gad-18 (canonical loop: snapshot → work → update → commit), gad-19 (GSD is dead, GAD only).
Delete .claude/commands/gsd/ directory entirely (30 command files).

Rebrand .claude/hooks/ for GAD: remove gsd-check-update.js (checks npm for get-shit-done-cc). Rename gsd-context-monitor.js → context-monitor.js, strip GSD references from warning messages. Rename gsd-statusline.js → statusline.js, strip GSD update check and branding. Update .claude/settings.json to reference new hook filenames.

Simplify root AGENTS.md: remove the 15-file mandatory read order. Agents use gad snapshot on session start, not manual file reads. Remove GSD section. Keep build/lint/verify gates, conventions, site copy guide. Reference vendor/get-anything-done/AGENTS.md as the canonical loop definition.

Simplify .planning/AGENTS.md: remove redundant loop definition, trigger phrase table, and pattern examples that duplicate root AGENTS.md and vendor GAD AGENTS.md. Keep XML schema reference and planning-config.toml pointers. Reference gad-18 for the loop.

Update vendor/get-anything-done/AGENTS.md: mark as canonical loop source per gad-18. Update "Current work" section to reference phase 12. Fix gad-17 reference to reflect auto-compact over fresh sessions.

Uninstall GSD globally: delete ~/.claude/get-shit-done/, ~/.claude/commands/gsd/ (56 files), ~/.claude/hooks/gsd-*.js (5 files). Clean ~/.claude/settings.json — remove all GSD hooks and statusline, keep permissions and preferences.

Install all 56 GAD commands from vendor/get-anything-done/commands/gad/ as Claude Code slash commands in .claude/commands/gad/. Includes the full planning loop: plan-phase, execute-phase, discuss-phase, progress, check-todos, next, plus project lifecycle, debug, health, verify, stats, and all other commands.

Clean all GSD/get-shit-done references in vendor/get-anything-done/commands/gad/*.md and propagate to .claude/commands/gad/. Replace GSD→GAD, get-shit-done→get-anything-done, /gsd:→/gad: throughout. Note: 115 references to get-shit-done remain as literal directory/file paths (the actual folder is named that).

skill_candidates.name: phase-13-docs-commands-skill-content-docs-map-rol
skill_candidates.source_phase_title: Docs commands, skill content, DOCS-MAP rollout

tasks.goal: Extend gad docs command: add `gad docs list --projectid <id>` that shows all docs for a project — planning files from sink status, DOCS-MAP entries, and docs.projects entries. Add --all flag.

Create DOCS-MAP.xml for get-anything-done with entries for evals.mdx and any other non-planning feature docs in the sink. Reader (docs-map-reader.cjs) already exists — this creates the actual data files.
Create DOCS-MAP.xml for grime-time, repo-planner, and any other roots that have feature docs in their sink paths. Check each root's sink directory for non-planning MDX files.
Flesh out write-intent SKILL.md: add concrete examples, template output, edge cases. The skill should be usable by any agent reading it cold — currently too abstract.
Flesh out write-feature-doc SKILL.md: add concrete examples of good feature docs, MDX frontmatter template, sink path resolution logic. Currently steps are correct but thin.
Flesh out write-tech-doc SKILL.md: add concrete examples, mermaid diagram patterns, decision-referencing conventions. Currently steps are correct but thin.
CLI error hardening: audit all gad.cjs commands for gad-12 compliance (missing-arg UX prints choices + rerun hint). Test edge cases: missing .planning/ dirs, malformed XML, empty files.
Run gad sink compile to sync the 7 stale files (get-anything-done and grime-time). Verify sink is fully up to date after phase 12 changes.

skill_candidates.name: phase-14-eval-framework-escape-the-dungeon-tracin
skill_candidates.source_phase_title: Eval framework: escape-the-dungeon + tracing

tasks.goal: Create a gad eval project "escape-the-dungeon" from GAMEPLAY-DESIGN.xml and STAT-AND-BEHAVIOUR-TAXONOMY.md. Convert these source docs into a GAD REQUIREMENTS.xml in the eval project directory (evals/escape-the-dungeon/). The requirements should be structured for an agent to plan phases and implement from — not a raw XML dump.

Create bin/gad-tools.cjs — the GAD equivalent of gsd-tools.cjs. Provides the core commands that workflows depend on: init (phase-op, execute-phase, plan-phase), commit, config-get, config-set, state (load, update, record-session), phase (find, add, complete), roadmap (get-phase, analyze), generate-slug, current-timestamp, scaffold, agent-skills. Reads from XML planning files instead of MD. This replaces all gsd-tools.cjs references in workflows.

Define the eval trace format for real implementations: track which gad commands were called, which gad skills were triggered, arguments passed, token counts, time-to-completion. Extend TRACE.json schema beyond the current unit-coverage model to include tool_calls[] and skill_triggers[].

Define the eval scoring rubric for real implementations: CLI efficiency (same as v6), skill trigger accuracy (were the right skills triggered at the right time), planning quality (task coverage, state hygiene), and time-to-completion. Human feedback is a separate manual score added after.

Run the escape-the-dungeon eval: fresh agent session, agent reads requirements, plans phases, implements using the GAD loop, traces everything. Produce TRACE.json and SCORE.md. This validates the full loop end-to-end on a real project.
Run the portfolio-bare eval with updated tracing. Compare against prior runs. Tighten portfolio-bare based on findings from the escape-the-dungeon eval.

Review CONTEXT.md: what it is, how discuss-phase produces it, how downstream agents (researcher, planner) consume it. Evaluate whether gad CLI should have a `gad context-doc` command or if gad-tools.cjs scaffold is sufficient. Decide if CONTEXT.md should be a GAD skill output or a planning file type. Update skills and CLI to integrate CONTEXT.md into the loop if valuable.

skill_candidates.name: phase-14.5-eval-findings-enforce-the-loop-automate-
skill_candidates.source_phase_title: Eval findings: enforce the loop, automate tracing, auto-scaffold conventions

tasks.goal: Create eval template AGENTS.md that enforces the GAD loop. The eval template's .planning/ directory must include an AGENTS.md that tells the agent: (1) run gad snapshot first, (2) plan phases in ROADMAP.xml before coding, (3) update TASK-REGISTRY.xml per task, (4) update STATE.xml after each phase, (5) capture decisions. This file is what the agent reads to know how to work — not a prompt instruction it ignores.

Update eval-run skill/command to inject snapshot context INTO the agent prompt. Instead of telling the agent "run gad snapshot", the eval-run workflow should run the snapshot itself and include the output in the agent's initial context. The agent starts with context, not instructions to get context.

Create gad eval trace reconstruct --project <name> command. Parses git log + planning doc state to build TRACE.json after the fact. Detects: which files were created/modified per commit, which planning docs were updated, time between commits (phase duration), task completion count from TASK-REGISTRY.xml diff. This replaces manual log-cmd/log-skill with post-hoc reconstruction.

Add checkpoint enforcement to execute-phase workflow. Before the agent can start the next task, it must have: (1) updated TASK-REGISTRY.xml for the previous task, (2) updated STATE.xml next-action. The workflow checks these preconditions. If not met, it prompts the agent to update before continuing. Not a hook — a step in the workflow that reads the planning docs and verifies.

Auto-scaffold CONVENTIONS.md after first implementation phase of greenfield projects. When phase 01 (or first phase with code output) completes in a project with no existing CONVENTIONS.md, run a lightweight codebase scan (file structure, naming patterns, import style, content pack format) and write .planning/CONVENTIONS.md. Integrate with gad:map-codebase skill output.

Define expected_triggers for implementation evals. The TRACE.json skill_accuracy field needs expected_triggers populated before the eval runs — defining which skills SHOULD fire and when. Create a template expected_triggers list for implementation evals: discuss-phase before planning, plan-phase before execution, execute-phase per phase, verify-work after phases, map-codebase after first implementation.

Re-run escape-the-dungeon eval with fixes applied (template AGENTS.md, snapshot injection, checkpoint enforcement). Compare v3 TRACE.json against v2. Validate that: gad CLI is actually used, task-level tracking happens, conventions are generated, trace has real data not backfilled.

skill_candidates.name: phase-17-reverse-engineer-skill-reader-workspace-
skill_candidates.source_phase_title: Reverse-engineer skill + reader-workspace eval

tasks.goal: Run gad:reverse-engineer on vendor/repub-builder/src/reader to produce REQUIREMENTS.xml, DECISIONS.xml, CONVENTIONS.md, ROADMAP.xml for the reader-workspace eval.
Create reader-workspace eval project with REQUIREMENTS.md, template/, and AGENTS.md.
Run reader-workspace eval v1: fresh agent builds the EPUB reader from reverse-engineered requirements alone. Produce TRACE.json and SCORE.md. Validates the full reverse-engineer → implement cycle.
Score reader-workspace v1: requirement coverage (51 criteria), architecture alignment (12 decisions), state hygiene, convention adherence. Compare output against reference implementation quality.

skill_candidates.name: phase-18-eval-suite-parallel-runs-iteration-cycle
skill_candidates.source_phase_title: Eval suite — parallel runs + iteration cycle

tasks.goal: Replace the stub in `gad eval run` with a prompt generator: read the eval template (AGENTS.md, REQUIREMENTS.xml, DECISIONS.xml, CONVENTIONS.md, ROADMAP.xml, STATE.xml), construct a full bootstrap prompt, and output it to stdout + eval-output.txt. The user/agent copies this prompt into their AI tool. Agent-agnostic per gad-01.

Add `gad eval suite` CLI command: lists all eval projects with templates, generates bootstrap prompts for each, outputs them as separate files in a timestamped suite run directory. Shows a summary table of what will be run.

Add `gad eval report` cross-project comparison: reads TRACE.json from latest run of each project, produces a comparison table (phases, tasks, discipline, scores) across all projects. Supports --all flag for all projects or specific --project list.

Add comprehensive logging: GAD CLI call logger (JSONL per day), PostToolUse hook for tool tracing (Bash/Read/Write/Edit/Agent/Skill/Grep/Glob), `gad log show|stats|clear` commands, `gad eval trace from-log` for definitive trace building from actual logs, GAD_LOG_DIR env for eval-scoped logs.
Run the full eval suite: portfolio-bare v3, escape-the-dungeon v4, reader-workspace v2. Launch all three in parallel worktree agents. Reconstruct traces and produce cross-project report.
Iterate on findings: review eval results, tighten at least one skill based on patterns, update eval-suite SKILL.md with lessons learned, add any new eval criteria discovered.

skill_candidates.name: phase-20-real-time-tooling-mcp-bridge-and-tooling
skill_candidates.source_phase_title: Real-time tooling, MCP bridge, and tooling evals

tasks.goal: Document tooling eval contract: extend TRACE.json with eval_type tooling|mcp, trace_schema_version, nested tooling or mcp metrics blocks, optional metrics.json for granular histograms. Record gad-26–gad-28 in DECISIONS.xml. Add a short section to vendor/get-anything-done/AGENTS.md (or evals/README) describing implementation vs tooling evals and how regressions are compared.

Implement `gad dev` or `gad watch planning`: debounced watch on .planning/**/*.xml (configurable), invoke existing refs verify logic, print one JSON summary line per run (ok, missing paths, timing) for scripts and terminal/CI consumers. Optional --poll for unreliable FS watchers. No network by default.

Add evals/tooling-watch-baseline (or equivalent): fixed fixture repo with known good/bad paths, automated Node harness that starts watcher, performs scripted edits, asserts correctness + latency percentiles, writes evals/<project>/vN/TRACE.json with eval_type tooling. Runnable in CI without an LLM.

Harden implementation eval scoring automation: generate scores.json alongside SCORE.md from score-generator; change gad eval scores to prefer JSON totals over SCORE.md regex. Prepare same pattern for tooling composite (tooling_composite) so weights can evolve without breaking old runs.

Optional MCP server package: thin adapter over the same core as CLI (verify, snapshot summary, session status). Stdio MCP; document Cursor config. Agents may use it; primary user remains human IDE workflows.
MCP tooling eval: golden tool invocations, cold-start and per-tool latency, error rate on bad args. Produces eval_type mcp TRACE.json; compare across gad releases.
IDE integration: document VS Code / Cursor Tasks (background watch), optional problem matcher mapping JSON errors to Problems panel. Update portfolio sink evals.mdx when behavior is stable.

Extend gad eval report and gad eval diff to include tooling/mcp runs (filter by eval_type, compare tooling.metrics and composite). Ensure suite or follow-up command can run implementation + tooling evals in one report.

skill_candidates.name: phase-21-eval-framework-evolution-v3-scoring-gate
skill_candidates.source_phase_title: Eval framework evolution: v3 scoring, gates, preservation contract, greenfield/brownfield

tasks.goal: Fix MCP UTF-8 buffer bug: Content-Length counts bytes but handleData used string.length. tools/list responses with em dashes broke the parser. Switch to Buffer throughout MCP harness and server.
Speed up planning-ref-verify by reading from planning-config roots instead of full tree walk, plus cache fs.statSync results.

Standardize implementation eval composite formula. Replace inconsistent per-eval weights with a single v3 formula: requirement_coverage + planning_quality + per_task_discipline + skill_accuracy + time_efficiency + human_review. Document in DEFINITIONS.md.
Clean skill_accuracy: remove methodology behaviors (decisions-captured, multi-phase-planning, phase-completion, task-lifecycle) from expected_triggers. Only actual /gad:* skill invocations count.
Add gad eval review CLI command to record human review scores that get folded into the composite with the v3 weights.
Add gad eval open CLI command to serve eval build output via local HTTP server (file:// doesn't work for ES module bundles from Vite/KAPLAY).
Create escape-the-dungeon-bare eval project: same game requirements, no GAD framework, agent creates own workflow. Baseline A/B comparison for the freedom hypothesis.
Create escape-the-dungeon-emergent eval project: no framework, but inherits skills from the previous run. Tests whether self-created systems improve iteratively.
Version game requirements. Document v1→v2→v3 history inside REQUIREMENTS.xml. Each eval round should reference the requirements version it used.

Build requirements v3: add gate criteria (game loop, spell crafting, UI quality) that override requirement_coverage to 0 if failed. Trim source gameplay design doc from 640 → 120 lines for the vertical slice focus.
Run round 2 greenfield evals with v2 requirements. Three conditions: GAD v7, Bare v2, Emergent v1.
Run round 3 greenfield evals with v3 requirements (gates). GAD v8, Bare v3, Emergent v2.
Document the freedom hypothesis finding: GAD framework constraint correlates inversely with creative output quality on greenfield game implementation. Capture in FINDINGS doc and decision.
Build eval preservation contract: gad eval preserve command that copies code+planning+build+logs to canonical per-version locations. Enforce via tests/eval-preservation.test.cjs.
Fix eval preserve to copy the ENTIRE project (not cherry-picked files). Agent may create files we don't anticipate.
Mandate game/.planning/ as the home for all eval workflow artifacts regardless of framework. Bare and emergent must put workflow files there too, not at project root.
Add gad worktree management commands: list, show, clean, prune. User had no visibility into .claude/worktrees/agent-* directories.
Categorize evals by eval_mode (greenfield|brownfield) and workflow (gad|bare|emergent). Show in gad eval list.

Create three brownfield eval projects (etd-brownfield-gad/bare/emergent) using bare v3 as baseline. Shared extension requirements (_brownfield-extensions-v4.md) describing v4 features (floor progression, respawning encounters, forge affinity, skills system, visual upgrade).
Port session tracking into TASK-REGISTRY.xml instead of Claude Code Task tool. Stop using ephemeral session tasks for GAD-tracked work.
Create an experiment log doc (evals/EXPERIMENT-LOG.md) that tracks every round: conditions, requirements version, results, findings. Running ledger across all rounds.

Build requirements v4: pressure-oriented design (not features checklist). Authored dungeon with floors → 5-8 rooms → boss gate. Room types: Combat, Elite, Forge, Rest, Event. Each floor must introduce a mechanical constraint that can't be brute-forced with default spells. Forge is gate with at least one encounter per floor that favors crafted/adapted spells. Pressure mechanics required from at least 2 of 4 categories. Starter abilities explicitly insufficient. Skills scored but not gate (combat needs non-spell action category only). Sprites: attempt sourcing first, coherent fallback allowed. Traits label in UI, narrativeStats in code. Add ingenuity/adaptation scoring dimension.

Create create_skill skill doc (skills/create-skill/SKILL.md) that documents how bare/emergent agents can define their own reusable skills. Only skill bare starts with; emergent starts with create_skill + inherited skills from previous runs.

Rewrite bare and emergent AGENTS.md to be truly minimal. Stop mandating ARCHITECTURE.md, CHANGELOG.md, or any specific artifact. Only mandate: (1) all workflow artifacts in game/.planning/, (2) reference create_skill skill for defining new skills. Everything else is agent-defined. This defeats the previous round's too-prescriptive guidance and lets us observe what emergent workflows actually produce.

Create gad:find-sprites skill (skills/find-sprites/SKILL.md) that documents the asset sourcing workflow: web search for free/open-license sprites and icons, preference order (npm packs → web search → generated → geometric fallback), integration tips, license verification. Referenced by v4 AGENTS.md for all 3 greenfield conditions.
Run round 4 greenfield with v4 requirements. Three conditions: GAD, Bare, Emergent. All with pressure-oriented gates.

Retry GAD greenfield v11 after HTTP 529 investigation. First attempt at v10 (tool_uses=55) completed phases 01+02 (scaffold + data layer) before Anthropic API returned overloaded_error. Previous attempt crashed earlier at tool_uses=18. Pattern suggests GAD's setup-heavy phases (snapshot + planning XML writes) spend proportionally more time in server-dependent states during peak load periods. Investigation needed: (1) check Anthropic status page for correlation with our crash timestamps; (2) test whether spreading the snapshot + plan calls across shorter API invocations reduces 529 exposure; (3) consider a retry-with-backoff wrapper in the eval runner for 529 specifically (not for rate limits). Only retry GAD v11 after investigation produces a mitigation or explicit "try again, the API was just overloaded that hour" conclusion.
Run round 1 brownfield experiments. Three conditions using bare v3 baseline + v4 extensions. BLOCKED on portfolio documentation (21-28).

skill_candidates.name: phase-22-portfolio-experiment-documentation-hub-p
skill_candidates.source_phase_title: Portfolio experiment documentation — hub, per-project, per-run, findings, graphs

tasks.goal: Plan the portfolio experiment documentation structure — pivoted to a standalone Next.js site at vendor/get-anything-done/site/ instead of apps/portfolio docs. Designed single-page sections (workflow, rounds, projects, results, playable, requirements, catalog, templates) plus dynamic per-run pages.
Hub/home page explaining the experiment, freedom hypothesis, methodology, and linking to every section.
Per-eval-project cards for all six projects with greenfield/brownfield split.
Per-run detail pages for every eval run with TRACE.json breakdown, dimension scores, gate report, process metrics, playable link.
Per-round experiment summary sections that compare conditions.

Visualizations/graphs across rounds. Deferred — score bars on the per-run page and run cards cover the "at a glance" comparison for now; a dedicated charts section can be revived later if we decide we're missing the signal.
Requirements version history (v1 → v4 lineage with diffs).

Decisions index page. Deferred — decisions referenced inline in Rounds and Requirements sections; full decisions index can be a dedicated /decisions route later when the list is big enough to warrant it.
Findings as standalone pages: /findings index + /findings/[slug] per finding.
Build pipeline so the site data stays in sync with the repo.
Extract v1 and v2 REQUIREMENTS.md snapshots from git history.
Markdown rendering via marked in prebuild.
Per-skill detail pages app/skills/[id]/page.tsx.
Per-agent detail pages app/agents/[id]/page.tsx.
Per-command detail pages app/commands/[id]/page.tsx.
Findings routes at /findings and /findings/[slug].
Framework vs eval-inherited catalog split.
/planning meta-transparency page.
skills/portfolio-sync/SKILL.md — methodology for keeping the public site in sync with evals.

Dedicated /gad framework overview page. Currently the home Loop + Catalog sections are entry points but there is no standalone "what is GAD" reference page someone can deep-link to. The catalog cards work but users have asked for a top-level GAD explainer + index that links into skills/agents/commands as a single navigation hub.

/methodology page that explains how tracing works, what fields TRACE.json captures, all scoring formulas per eval project (pulled from evals/<project>/gad.json), the low-score caps from v3, and the gate logic. This is the "how we measure" appendix users have been asking for. Includes concrete worked examples showing how a composite score was derived.

Per-run page enhancements: (1) render composite formula as a weighted-sum breakdown showing each dimension's contribution to the composite; (2) when skill_accuracy has expected_triggers array (like v5), render the per-skill trigger table; (3) when skill_accuracy is a bare number (like v8), show a "tracing gap" callout noting the run didn't capture per-skill detail and link to phase 25 (trace schema overhaul).

Per-project detail pages at /projects/[id]. Shows: project description + mode + workflow, the catalog scope for that project (framework GAD = full catalog, bare = 2 bootstrap skills, emergent = 2 bootstrap + inherited), all runs with inline scores, "what the agent built for itself" per run (scan run/.planning/skills/*.md and similar).

Lightweight SVG charts in a Graphs section on the home page: composite + human review bars per run grouped by workflow, rounds timeline, freedom hypothesis scatter (composite vs human review divergence). No charting library — pure React-rendered SVG so it ships in every prebuild.
Nav surface update: add Framework + Methodology + Projects links. Current nav is already packed — may need a secondary nav row or drop items.

Expose scoring weights per project from evals/<project>/gad.json in the generated data. Every eval project can have different weights; we're currently only reading one set for display. Emit SCORING_WEIGHTS by project so the methodology page and per-run formula display can show the project-specific math.

New skill skills/framework-upgrade/SKILL.md. Methodology for versioning the GAD framework on branches (version/v1.x), stamping every TRACE.json with the framework commit it ran against, re-running past evals when the framework changes to check for framework-level score drift. References phase 25 as the backing work.
Mark the create-skill inheritance requirement explicit in a DECISION so bare/emergent templates are not accidentally regressed.

/lineage page explaining context rot, the GSD upstream, the RepoPlanner predecessor (b2gdevs / https://repo-planner.vercel.app/ — first Ralph Wiggum loop formalization), and how GAD combines both approaches plus measurement. Links to both projects' sources. Captures decision gad-56.

Agent Runtimes comparison table on /methodology showing which coding agents can produce trace v4 data (Claude Code ✓ via hooks, Aider ✓ via Python callbacks, Continue.dev ✓ via extension API, Cursor ✗, vanilla Codex CLI ✗ stream-only). Published alongside decision gad-53 explicit note that agents without hook runtimes are unsupported for GAD evaluation.

Phase 26 scaffolding: install @remotion/player, create site/remotion/ directory with Root.tsx composition registry + one placeholder Composition.tsx (the v8 dissection stub), create app/videos/page.tsx index with filter, create components/VideoEmbed.tsx inline player wrapper (30s cap, cinematic passthrough, stop button, reuses existing site components as frames). Does NOT author full video content — the scaffold establishes the pattern and the first composition is a placeholder.
Write .planning/DISCUSS-PHASE-25.md capturing the trace schema v4 discussion output.

`gad install` CLI subcommand as a local-checkout-friendly wrapper around existing bin/install.js. The underlying install.js already supports --claude --cursor --codex --opencode --gemini --copilot --antigravity --windsurf --augment --all --uninstall and ships the full GSD-pattern cross-runtime install — but it's hardcoded for the npm-published package layout (expects to find files under `node_modules/get-anything-done/get-anything-done/`). This task wraps it in a `gad install` subcommand that resolves paths correctly whether running from a git checkout (vendor/get-anything-done/) or an npm install. Related to 22-34 (fix install.js directly) — this task may become a no-op if 22-34 fixes the path resolution at the install.js level.
Fix path resolution bug in bin/install.js for local git checkouts.
Design trace event envelope + 4 event types as a reusable library in lib/trace-schema.cjs.
Write the hook handler script at bin/gad-trace-hook.cjs that Claude Code invokes for PreToolUse/PostToolUse.
4 KB truncation helper with head+tail split for trace event outputs.
`gad install hooks` CLI subcommand that wires the trace hook into Claude Code settings.json.

Fix YAML parsing errors in session-authored skill frontmatter (create-skill, objective-eval-design). Descriptions with embedded colons like "methodology:" and "discipline:" were parsed by js-yaml as key-value separators, breaking skill loading in Claude Code. Fix by converting plain scalar descriptions to YAML folded block scalar form (`description: >- ...`) which treats the body as opaque.

Dogfood-failure recovery: author skills/emergent/session-discipline/SKILL.md as an agent-authored hard-start checklist. Move agent-authored skills into skills/emergent/ so default install can skip them. Add origin + authored-by + authored-on + excluded-from-default-install fields to CatalogSkill. Capture gad-78 (dogfood failure) + gad-79 (task_pressure programmatic) decisions.

Reorganize .planning/ root: move ad-hoc markdown docs (ASSUMPTIONS.md, GAPS.md, SKEPTIC.md, SESSION-REFLECTION-*.md) into a new .planning/docs/ subdirectory per user directive. Rationale: .planning/ root should hold the formal XML artifacts that gad snapshot surfaces; ad-hoc human-written docs that are not formal (but still project-relevant) belong in a peer docs/ folder. Update all site page references to the new paths.

Retroactively update TASK-REGISTRY.xml to mark 22-20/22-21/22-23/22-24/22-25/22-29 as done (work shipped in prior sessions without registry updates — dogfood failure per gad-78). Add new tasks 22-36/22-37/22-38/22-39/22-40/22-41/22-42/22-43 for this session's work.

Agent-crawlable documentation export: emit public/llms.txt (concise index per llmstxt.org convention), public/llms-full.txt (full-text dump of skills/decisions/questions/bugs/glossary/tasks/phases/current-requirements), public/api/docs.json (structured JSON with schema gad-docs-v1) at prebuild. Provides a full API reference for external agents evaluating or reviewing GAD without needing to crawl HTML.

Interactive chart on /roadmap showing human review across rounds broken out by hypothesis track. Solid lines for tracks with real data (freedom/csh/gad). Dashed ghost lines for planned tracks without runs yet (content-driven, codex runtime) so the research plan is visible.

/hypotheses index page + /freedom evidence page + /content-driven planned-hypothesis page. Every hypothesis the project tracks gets wired to its eval track, its latest evidence, and its dedicated evidence page. Status labels: preliminary observation / discussing / operationalized / not-yet-tested.

Clickable skills modal on /emergent: convert the static skill-artifact chip list on the emergent page into client-component buttons that open a modal showing the full SKILL.md content with Copy button + GitHub source link. Per user directive 2026-04-09: "I wish i could click and download or get that pop up of those skills on the page."

Scaffold evals/gad-explainer-video/ as a new eval project: REQUIREMENTS.md describing the Remotion GAD explainer video task (3-minute explainer, 4 mandatory gate frames, pedagogical clarity as primary rubric dimension), gad.json with workflow:gad + compare_to bare/emergent siblings + rubric weights. Tests freedom/CSH/emergent-evolution hypotheses on a DIFFERENT task domain (video composition instead of game implementation) — the generalization test /skeptic says we need.

Manual fresh-clone contribution test (open question fresh-clone-contribution-test, decision gad-77). Clone the repo into a new directory, walk through the /contribute 5-step flow (clone → install → open in Claude → talk conversationally), document everything that breaks, update /contribute with the friction points, remove the "fresh-clone test still open" amber warning. Goal: make contribution actually work for a human who has never touched GAD before.

Site/remotion/ runtime scaffold (second half of task 22-31): create Root.tsx composition registry, one placeholder Composition.tsx, site/app/videos/page.tsx loading compositions via @remotion/player, VideoEmbed.tsx wrapper component. This unblocks the gad-explainer-video eval project (22-43) being run for the first time.

Adopt agentskills.io .agents/skills/ convention (gad-80). Extend bin/install.js to copy skills to BOTH .claude/skills/ (native) AND .agents/skills/ (cross-client) for supported runtimes. Migrate the fundamental triumvirate (create-skill, merge-skill, find-skills per gad-73) to the new location once those skills exist. Required for the codex-vs-claude comparison eval (task 89) because Codex uses different discovery conventions — if we target .agents/skills/, both runtimes find the same skills. Also honor the excluded-from-default-install:true frontmatter flag I added to the emergent skill — the installer must skip those.

Build a /standards page on the site citing both the Anthropic skills guide (resources.anthropic.com/hubfs/The-Complete-Guide-to-Building-Skill-for-Claude.pdf) and agentskills.io. Link from /skills, /security, /methodology, /gad. The page must summarize: (a) SKILL.md format + required frontmatter, (b) progressive disclosure three-tier loading, (c) name collision handling, (d) the per-skill evaluation methodology (with_skill vs without_skill baseline per agentskills.io/skill-creation/evaluating-skills). This becomes the reference page every skill-related section of the site cites instead of repeating the content inline.

Worktree isolation audit script (gad-82, open question worktree-isolation-verification). Write scripts/audit-worktree-isolation.mjs that walks a fresh eval worktree and reports: which files from the parent repo are reachable (by relative path traversal), what settings from .claude/settings.json inherit, what env vars are set, what's on PATH, which workspace skills at vendor/get-anything-done/skills/ could be picked up by a scan that walks parent dirs. Output structured report. ROUND 5 IS BLOCKED on either passing this audit or documenting which parent-repo state is explicitly allowed (e.g. the trace hook handler is allowed because it's the instrumentation layer per gad-50; workspace skills are NOT allowed because bare condition requires zero framework affordances).

Skill collision detection at prebuild (gad-81). Scan every skills/*/SKILL.md description for overlapping trigger keywords. Compute a keyword profile per skill, find overlaps above a threshold, emit a collision report to data/skill-collisions.json. Add a "collision candidates" section to the /skills site page showing any detected overlaps with a merge-skill suggestion link (merge-skill itself not yet built — link falls back to a manual-review prompt). Also: a keyword index for every skill so users searching can see what terms trigger each.

Extract thinking blocks from session.jsonl into TRACE.json derived.reasoning_trace (gad-84, GAPS G13). Post-hoc offline enrichment — no new hooks required. Walk a run's session.jsonl, pull every thinking block and inter-tool message text, attach to TRACE.json with timestamps tying thoughts to subsequent tool uses. First exploratory analysis: thought-to-action interval, thought length distribution, keyword presence (exploration vs execution), thought-before-action vs thought-after-action ordering. This is the highest-leverage way to get "agent reasoning" signal without Claude Code exposing new hook surfaces.

Scaffold new eval domain evals/skill-evaluation-app/ per decision gad-88. Third task-domain alongside escape-the-dungeon (game dev) and gad-explainer-video (video composition). Tests the same hypotheses (freedom / CSH / emergent-evolution) on GUI app development. The GUI, once built, is also part of the GAD eval framework (dogfood).

Build the GAD-native per-skill evaluation harness: gad eval skill <name> CLI subcommand. Reads evals/evals.json inside the skill directory, spawns clean-context subagent runs (with_skill + without_skill) via gad worktree, captures trace events from v4 schema (gad-50) as the observable signal, grades assertions by querying trace events + file mutations + commit log (NOT LLM self-report — that's what skill-creator's Python harness does and it didn't work for us), emits benchmark.json compatible with agentskills.io format. This is the load-bearing piece that lets every GAD skill graduate from experimental to canonical per gad-86. Directly answers programmatic-eval GAPS G2 + the hygiene half of G11.

Author merge-skill + find-skills SKILL.md files as workspace skills. find-skills is a thin methodology wrapper around npx skills (vercel-labs/skills CLI per gad-85). merge-skill is a pure methodology doc for fusing overlapping skills. Both start as experimental (status: experimental) because no evaluation harness has been run against them yet.

Landing page + /hypotheses interactive chart. User directive 2026-04-09: "the interactive chart I asked for should have been on the front page, but I dont see it... I still see an emergent page, but not a hypothesis page with graphs and etc detailing it out visually." Move or duplicate the HypothesisTracksChart from /roadmap onto the landing page (after Hero, before WhatItIs) and add it to /hypotheses above the hypothesis cards.

skill_candidates.name: phase-30-eval-framework-v2-domains-tech-stacks-ro
skill_candidates.source_phase_title: Eval framework v2 — domains, tech stacks, rounds per project

tasks.goal: gad rounds --project shows per-project rounds derived from TRACE.json requirements_version changes across runs.
Build requirement gate — gad eval score checks if build requirement (from gad.json) was produced. Failed gate = eval incomplete.
Human time estimation in eval prompt — buildEvalPrompt() includes instruction for agent to estimate human_estimate_hours before coding.
Source + build size computation — gad eval preserve computes source_size_bytes and build_size_bytes, writes to TRACE.json.
Site project detail shows domain badge + tech stack from eval project metadata.

skill_candidates.name: phase-34-executable-release-pipeline-github-first
skill_candidates.source_phase_title: Executable release pipeline + GitHub-first distribution
skill_candidates.reviewed_notes: test discard — phase 34 release work is one-off, not a reusable pattern

tasks.goal: Define the executable packaging target for gad: Windows, macOS, and Linux standalone binaries with no Node requirement. Choose and document the packaging toolchain, output artifact names, signing/notarization assumptions, and which runtime files (CLI bundle, hooks, templates, skills) must ship alongside the executable.

Implement the official GitHub-first install flow in docs and CLI messaging. Primary path: GitHub Release executable. Secondary path: direct GitHub repo/Node install. Remove or rewrite remaining npm-first copy in quick start, README, starter READMEs, and site pages.

Publish installable GAD skills through skills.sh / `npx skills add` using the GitHub repo as the source of truth. Document the exact command(s), verify the repo is listable/installable from skills.sh, and make skill installation distinct from CLI installation throughout the docs.

Build a path-gated release workflow so executable rebuild/release work only triggers on shipped CLI surface changes (bin/, lib/, hooks/, package.json, build/release scripts, and bundled assets). Non-shipping docs/site/eval/planning commits must not require a new release.

Implement the release pipeline: Windows authoritative local build, optional GitHub Actions builds for macOS/Linux, and GitHub Release asset publication/update. Document the operator flow for releasing after CLI-shipping changes.
